project_baseline.delivery_note: Main advanced during delivery through the commercial polish, organization migration, and archive cleanup commit; the audited architecture surfaces remain applicable.
non_negotiables: No external send without founder approval
No second orchestration runtime without a demonstrated gap
No dependency adoption without license, security, rollback, and evidence gates

decision_scale.adopt_now: Dependency-free integration or hardening of an existing Dealix surface
decision_scale.pilot: Isolated, reversible evaluation with fake or public data
decision_scale.pattern_only: Learn from the architecture without importing code/runtime
decision_scale.connector_only: Offer an optional boundary integration; do not embed as Dealix core
decision_scale.hold: Wait for stable release, license clarity, or security review
decision_scale.reject_core: Do not add to the core because it duplicates the runtime or violates trust/economics

items.name: OpenAI Agents SDK for Python
items.july_signal: 0.17.8 through 0.18.3 added/fixed strict handoffs, sessions, tracing, realtime usage, provider isolation, bounded schema references and hosted multi-agent controls
items.integration: Reuse tracing-span, bounded-schema and provider-isolation patterns behind the existing ModelRouter; do not replace the router or add a second agent runtime.
gates: founder approval before paid API activation

items.name: OpenAI GPT-5.6 family
items.july_signal: Sol, Terra and Luna with Responses API programmatic tool calling and hosted multi-agent beta
items.integration: Add only as an optional provider adapter and compare Arabic quality, latency and cost against the current GLM/local route.

items.name: Pydantic AI
items.july_signal: 2.10 through 2.14.1 added background/deferred events, durable tool IDs and Temporal/DBOS/Prefect durability; July also included an AG-UI sanitization security advisory
items.integration: Prototype one resumable approval-bound commercial workflow using existing schemas; do not migrate the whole runtime.

items.name: Google Agent Development Kit 2.0
items.july_signal: Structured workflow runtime, deterministic routing, strict state boundaries, HITL and task collaboration
items.integration: Adopt the graph boundary: deterministic code owns routing and permissions; LLM nodes only classify, reason or draft.
gates: approval node before every external action

items.name: LangGraph
items.july_signal: 1.2.8 fixed fresh-thread updateState snapshot behavior; CLI 0.4.31 updated deployment images
items.integration: Keep checkpoint/resume patterns as a reference. Existing Dealix loops must demonstrate a failure before runtime adoption.

items.name: FastMCP
items.july_signal: 3.4.3 hardened SSRF, DNS rebinding, OAuth redirects and task argument validation; 3.4.4 relaxed host/origin defaults for compatibility
items.integration: If Dealix upgrades or adopts FastMCP, explicitly configure trusted hosts/origins and preserve its current gateway; never rely on relaxed defaults.

items.name: MCP TypeScript SDK 2.0 beta
items.july_signal: 2.0.0-beta.4 split server, legacy, framework, core, codemod and client packages
items.integration: Track package split and prepare a migration note only; do not put a beta SDK on the production path.

items.name: Playwright MCP
items.july_signal: 0.0.78 added browser_find, distilled snapshots, mobile/device emulation, status codes and sandbox fixes
items.integration: Use only in isolated QA with a disposable browser profile; feed evidence into the existing E2E proof manifest.

items.name: Browser Use
items.july_signal: 0.13.3-0.13.6 added CLI 3.0/Browser Harness, shared MCP runner, navigation reliability and MCP registry support
items.integration: Evaluate only for public-source research and QA. Playwright remains the deterministic default.

items.name: browser-control
items.july_signal: New local relay/extension lets coding agents control an existing logged-in browser profile
items.integration: Do not deploy in server, CI or customer workflows because ambient logged-in authority violates least privilege.

items.name: MCP Trust Checker
items.july_signal: New offline deterministic MCP scanner claiming 78 rules and 304 tests
items.integration: Run as a non-authoritative offline check against candidate MCP manifests; Dealix policy remains the deciding gate.

items.name: Nango
items.july_signal: 0.71.0-0.71.1 added many integrations, egress controls, audit boundaries, MCP Auth guidance and fixes
items.integration: Use only as an internal self-hosted or paid external gateway after legal review; do not resell its substantial functionality as embedded Dealix core.

items.name: Langfuse
items.july_signal: 3.220-3.221 improved trace tables/charts, dashboard assistance, retention handling, auth callbacks and sandbox egress
items.integration: Harden the existing integration: redact before export, batch/retry, explicit egress, trace lineage and version compatibility. Do not add Phoenix concurrently.
gates: failure does not block business flow

items.name: Arize Phoenix
items.july_signal: 19.0-19.1 added OAuth2 auth server, CLI login, evaluation regression gates and MCP setup
items.integration: Borrow regression-gate ideas only. Langfuse already occupies the observability slot.
gates: only revisit after a measured Langfuse gap

items.name: n8n
items.july_signal: 2.30-2.31 delivered credential validation, community-package provenance lint, SES signing and reliability fixes
items.integration: Optional external execution plane after credential isolation; never grant direct Dealix production DB access or external-send autonomy.

items.name: Twenty CRM
items.july_signal: 2.18-2.22 added call-recording/Fireflies objects, workflow tooling and auth/draft-send fixes
items.integration: Do not replace Dealix or embed a second CRM. Support read-only/customer-owned integration only when demanded.

items.name: OpenNous
items.july_signal: New GTM context graph organizes immutable observations into entities and claims with confidence/freshness plus MCP access
items.integration: Clean-room design: add observation -> entity -> claim with confidence, freshness and source hash to Dealix-native schemas; do not copy AGPL code.
gates: legal review if service separation is considered

items.name: Agent Pulse
items.july_signal: New evidence-backed AI industry radar using fact/evidence/context/impact/next-signal records
items.integration: Adopt the evidence schema and source tiers in the dependency-free monthly radar; never auto-promote a social claim to product truth.

items.name: Capital One VulnHunter
items.july_signal: Initial July launch with hunt -> falsify -> fix -> independent verify workflow
items.integration: Use only in an isolated, explicitly authorized security lab; start with the read-only verifier and treat findings as hypotheses until reproduced.

items.name: Ollama
items.july_signal: 0.31.2-0.32.1 added an interactive agent, older-GPU flash attention, safer GGUF handling, better tool calling and cache fixes; launch warns on older Qwen2.5 model tags
items.integration: Regression-test an Ollama upgrade locally. Keep the existing 7B route until an actually licensed replacement fits the 7 GB machine and wins the Arabic/code eval.

items.name: vLLM
items.july_signal: 0.25 made Model Runner V2 default, expanded model/parser/speculative decoding support and 0.25.1 fixed two launch/correctness bugs
items.integration: No fit for the current 7 GB local machine or Railway/Vercel app path; revisit only with dedicated GPU economics.

items.name: llama.cpp
items.july_signal: Rapid July builds added/fixed multiple CPU/GPU backends, quantization and model-cache paths
items.integration: Consume through a pinned Ollama release rather than managing a second local inference engine.

items.name: AWS Labs Loom
items.july_signal: New control plane with identity/scopes, MCP/A2A, memory, approvals and cost controls on AWS AgentCore/Strands
items.integration: Borrow scoped identity and cost-control patterns only; AWS-specific runtime conflicts with the current Railway/Vercel architecture.

items.name: TencentCloud Octop
items.july_signal: New self-hosted multi-user assistant with memory, connectors, browser and shell capabilities
items.integration: Reject wholesale adoption: it is a parallel runtime and expands shell/browser authority.

items.name: AgentSmith
items.july_signal: New portable documentation/rules harness for coding agents
items.integration: Compare its portable rule layout with current Dealix agent instructions; adopt only missing verification patterns.

items.name: Browser Memory (bmem)
items.july_signal: Open catalog of reusable web skills for agents
items.integration: Treat as an untrusted recipe source. Manually review and translate useful steps into Dealix-native, domain-allowlisted playbooks.

items.name: Memmy Agent
items.july_signal: New local-first cross-agent memory desktop/CLI/API project
items.integration: Use only memory-boundary and user-control patterns. Do not ingest private coding sessions or raw browser history.

items.name: Open Kritt
items.july_signal: New agentic security research orchestrator using containers, root access and direct internet
items.integration: Never run inside Dealix production or the founder workstation; only a separate authorized disposable VM if ever needed.

items.name: fastctx
items.july_signal: New Rust MCP tools aimed at context-efficient code operations
items.integration: Do not install until a license file, reproducible build and security review are available.

items.name: GitHub Copilot July changes
items.july_signal: Browser tools GA, vision GA, task-routed model selection, session streaming, OTel export and agentic code-scanning autofix preview
items.integration: Enable only at developer-account level with session budgets and OTel/usage review; autofix remains draft/PR-only.
